message:
fix(reactor): handle ENOENT and EBADF gracefully in kqueue modify

Make KqueueReactor::modify() resilient to stale registrations by
handling OS errors that indicate the fd is no longer tracked by kqueue:

- Switch from get_mut() to entry() API so we can remove the
  registration atomically on failure without a second lookup.
- On ENOENT (fd not in kqueue): remove the stale registration entry
  and return NotFound, keeping bookkeeping in sync with the kernel.
- On EBADF: check if the fd is actually closed (fcntl F_GETFD). If
  the fd is gone, remove the entry and return NotFound. If the fd is
  still valid, propagate the original error (indicates a different
  problem).
- On success: update the interest field via entry.into_mut() as before.

This prevents registration map drift when fds are closed concurrently
with modify calls, which can happen during connection teardown races.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
